content.content:
- content: 'Internal notes for Slack enterprise configuration changes:


    Workspace URL and primary-domain preparation can start with the workspace

    owner path when the user has access to the Slack admin console. DNS

    verification may require coordination with the DNS administrator.


    A data-location or data-residency region change requires an approved Legal

    & Compliance record before the change is initiated. The request must be

    sponsored by the Primary Owner or by another person formally designated as

    an enterprise-configuration sponsor under the organization''s approval

    process.


    After approval, the sponsor must follow the handling procedure specified

    by Slack''s current official data-residency guidance. This internal policy

    does not define the external product channel or interface used for the

    change.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: Internal Slack Enterprise Configuration Controls
  url: null
- content: 'Billing-route notes for SaaS workspaces:


    Marketplace and reseller contacts handle billing or contract questions

    only when the workspace is billed through those channels. A direct-vendor

    contract means that marketplace and reseller contacts are not the correct

    destination for this workspace.


    Billing route alone does not determine the handling procedure for a

    configuration change. Use the current official product guidance to

    determine the applicable procedure.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 2
  title: Internal SaaS Billing Route Notes
  url: null

args.query: site:slack.com/help data residency change region enterprise Slack contact support

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Change your workspace or org name and URL | Slack

  - url: https://slack.com/help/articles/201663443-Change-your-workspace-or-org-name-and-URL

  - source: Slack Help Center

  - domain: slack.com


  ## Content


  # Change your workspace or org name and URL


  Slack explains that a workspace name appears in menus and headings, while the workspace
  URL is the web address members use to access Slack. A workspace URL can be updated,
  but members should be told about the change. If a workspace URL changes, related
  services such as single sign-on and connected apps may also need their Slack URL
  references updated.


  ## Change a workspace name or URL


  For Free, Pro, and Business+ workspaces, Workspace Owners can change the workspace
  name and URL from Slack:


  1. Open Slack on desktop.

  2. Click **Admin** in the sidebar.

  3. Select **Edit workspace**, then open **Name and URL**.

  4. Choose **Change Workspace Name & URL**.

  5. Enter the new workspace name or workspace URL.

  6. Save the changes.


  Slack notes that the old workspace URL becomes available for another group to use.
  Slack may redirect the old address to the new one while the old address has not
  been claimed by another group.


  ## Enterprise organization name or URL


  For Enterprise plans, an Enterprise organization can have both workspace-level names
  and URLs and an overarching organization name and URL. Workspace Owners can change
  a workspace name and URL from the workspace settings path. Org Owners can change
  the organization name and URL from Organization settings.


  For a workspace-level name or URL change on Enterprise, Workspace Owners use the
  workspace menu, go to **Tools & settings**, then **Workspace settings**, find **Workspace
  Name & URL**, and select **Change Workspace Name & URL**.


  For an org-level name or URL change, Org Owners open the organization menu, go to
  **Tools & settings**, then **Organization settings**, open **Settings**, choose
  **Organization Info**, edit the details, enter the new organization name or domain,
  and save.


  ## Who can use this feature?


  Slack lists Workspace Owners and Org Owners / Org Admins for this feature. It is
  available on all plans.


  '
metadata: {}
title: Change your workspace or org name and URL | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/201663443-Change-your-workspace-or-org-name-and-URL

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Data residency for Slack | Slack\n\
  - url: https://slack.com/help/articles/360035633934-Data-residency-for-Slack\n-\
  \ source: Slack Help Center\n- domain: slack.com\n\n## Content\n\n# Data residency\
  \ for Slack\n\nSlack data residency lets eligible customers choose the region where\
  \ certain categories of customer data at rest are stored. Data residency is available\
  \ on Business+ and Enterprise plans, and each organization uses a single selected\
  \ data region at a time.\n\n## Available regions\n\nSlack lists multiple data residency\
  \ regions and backup regions, including regions in Germany, France, Sweden, Switzerland,\
  \ Japan, India, Singapore, South Korea, Australia, the United Kingdom, Canada, and\
  \ Brazil. Some regions use a backup location in the same country or region, while\
  \ others use a nearby backup region.\n\n## Data stored in the selected region\n\n\
  When data residency is enabled, selected customer data is stored at rest in a data\
  \ center within the selected region from the date data residency is enabled. Slack\
  \ lists categories such as messages, canvases, snippets, uploaded files, search\
  \ indexes of Customer Data, app- or bot-generated messages and files, and certain\
  \ data from apps deployed to Slack-managed infrastructure.\n\nSlack also notes that\
  \ some flagged or reported customer data may be stored in the United States to enforce\
  \ Slack\u2019s Acceptable Use Policy.\n\n## Data stored outside the selected region\n\
  \nSlack explains that some categories may still be stored outside the selected customer\
  \ data region. Examples include Slack member profiles, workspace and channel membership\
  \ information, data used to measure seat count, usage and revenue, analytics and\
  \ quality-of-service data such as sanitized logs, IDs generated by Slack, and deal\
  \ celebration data.\n\n## New organizations and initial enablement\n\nFor a new\
  \ workspace or Enterprise organization created in a data residency region, eligible\
  \ customer data is stored in the selected region from creation. For an existing\
  \ organization enabling data residency for the first time, newly generated eligible\
  \ data is stored in the selected region after enablement. Migration of older data\
  \ is a separate operation.\n\n## Changing an existing data residency region\n\n\
  For an existing Enterprise Grid organization that already has data residency enabled,\
  \ changing the selected data residency region is not completed in Organization Settings\
  \ or in the workspace URL/domain workflow. After the organization\u2019s required\
  \ approvals are complete, the Primary Owner or another authorized enterprise contact\
  \ must work with Slack through the organization\u2019s enterprise account or support\
  \ channel to request and coordinate the region change.\n\nThe handling rule in this\
  \ section applies specifically to a change from one selected data residency region\
  \ to another. It does not change the ordinary workspace name, workspace URL, organization\
  \ name, or organization URL procedures.\n\n## Who can use this feature?\n\nSlack\
  \ lists data residency as available on Business+ and Enterprise plans. The organization\u2019\
  s authorized owner or enterprise contact is responsible for initiating changes under\
  \ the applicable procedure.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Data residency for Slack | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/360035633934-Data-residency-for-Slack

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Contact Us | Slack
- url: https://slack.com/help/requests/new
\
  - source: Slack Help Center
- domain: slack.com

## Content

# Contact Us
\
  
Slack’s Contact Us page is a general support entry point. Slack asks existing\
  \ Slack users to sign in so Slack can tailor the support experience. If signing\
  \ in is not possible, the page still allows the user to contact Slack.

The page\
  \ may show an in-progress chat, a confirmation that a chat transcript was emailed,\
  \ or links to the Help Center and support history. Slack also provides options to\
  \ get answers quickly through an AI support agent or by contacting support.

\
  ## Support request topics

The page includes a topic selector with categories\
  \ such as:

- Audio & video
- Billing & plans
- Connection trouble
- Managing\
  \ channels
- Managing members
- Notifications
- Signing in
- Slack Connect
\
  - Workflow Builder
- Workspace migration

The page also includes a free-text\
  \ field where the user can describe what they need help with.

## Support history\
  \ and sign-in

Slack links support to the user’s Slack account when possible.\
  \ The page includes options to sign in, see support history, and visit the Help\
  \ Center.

## Scope of this page

This page is a broad support intake page.\
  \ It does not itself determine whether a workspace URL, domain claim, data residency\
  \ setup, enterprise data-location request, billing-route issue, or internal compliance-controlled\
  \ change should be handled by a Workspace Owner, Org Owner, Primary Owner, contract\
  \ owner, legal/compliance approver, DNS administrator, marketplace/reseller contact,\
  \ or Slack enterprise support path.

For enterprise configuration changes, this\
  \ page should not be treated as proof that any generic support topic is the correct\
  \ destination for the current case.
"
metadata: {}
title: Contact Us | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/requests/new

Your request needs to be handled as **two separate workstreams**:

1. **Workspace URL / primary-domain change — Slack Admin**
   - The active change is **WCC-48291**.
   - You are a **Workspace Owner**, so you can continue the workspace-level URL change in Slack’s admin settings.
   - It is currently blocked because the DNS administrator has not published the required **TXT verification record** for `examplecorp.com`.
   - **Next step:** ask your DNS administrator to publish the TXT record, then return to the active URL/domain change and complete verification.
   - Slack’s procedure: [Change your workspace or org name and URL](https://slack.com/help/articles/201663443-Change-your-workspace-or-org-name-and-URL)

2. **Data-residency region change — Enterprise approval and Slack enterprise support**
   - The data-residency change is included in the request scope, but it **cannot be completed through the URL/domain workflow or Organization Settings**.
   - Because this is an existing Enterprise Grid organization with residency already enabled, it requires:
     1. An approved **Legal & Compliance record**.
     2. Sponsorship by the **Primary Owner** or a formally designated enterprise-configuration sponsor.
     3. Coordination with Slack through your organization’s **enterprise account/support channel**.
   - Your internal approval status is currently **pending**, and no approval reference is recorded. You are not the Primary Owner, Legal approver, or contract owner, so do not submit the residency request as though you have that authority.
   - Slack guidance: [Data residency for Slack](https://slack.com/help/articles/360035633934-Data-residency-for-Slack)

**Recommended immediate sequence**

- Have the DNS administrator publish the verification TXT record.
- Ask your Primary Owner or governance team to identify the authorized sponsor and create/approve the Legal & Compliance record for the residency-region change.
- Once that approval exists, the authorized sponsor should contact the Slack enterprise account/support channel to coordinate the region change separately.
- Keep the URL/domain change and residency-region change tracked as separate requests, even if they are related operationally.

final_answer:
Your request needs to be handled as **two separate workstreams**:

1. **Workspace URL / primary-domain change — Slack Admin**
   - The active change is **WCC-48291**.
   - You are a **Workspace Owner**, so you can continue the workspace-level URL change in Slack’s admin settings.
   - It is currently blocked because the DNS administrator has not published the required **TXT verification record** for `examplecorp.com`.
   - **Next step:** ask your DNS administrator to publish the TXT record, then return to the active URL/domain change and complete verification.
   - Slack’s procedure: [Change your workspace or org name and URL](https://slack.com/help/articles/201663443-Change-your-workspace-or-org-name-and-URL)

2. **Data-residency region change — Enterprise approval and Slack enterprise support**
   - The data-residency change is included in the request scope, but it **cannot be completed through the URL/domain workflow or Organization Settings**.
   - Because this is an existing Enterprise Grid organization with residency already enabled, it requires:
     1. An approved **Legal & Compliance record**.
     2. Sponsorship by the **Primary Owner** or a formally designated enterprise-configuration sponsor.
     3. Coordination with Slack through your organization’s **enterprise account/support channel**.
   - Your internal approval status is currently **pending**, and no approval reference is recorded. You are not the Primary Owner, Legal approver, or contract owner, so do not submit the residency request as though you have that authority.
   - Slack guidance: [Data residency for Slack](https://slack.com/help/articles/360035633934-Data-residency-for-Slack)

**Recommended immediate sequence**

- Have the DNS administrator publish the verification TXT record.
- Ask your Primary Owner or governance team to identify the authorized sponsor and create/approve the Legal & Compliance record for the residency-region change.
- Once that approval exists, the authorized sponsor should contact the Slack enterprise account/support channel to coordinate the region change separately.
- Keep the URL/domain change and residency-region change tracked as separate requests, even if they are related operationally.
